premises.premise: Theoretical calculations predict the crystal structures and lattice parameters of body-centred tetragonal ($bct$) $YH_{4}$, body-centred cubic ($bcc$) $YH_{6}$, and hexagonal close packed ($hcp$) $YH_{9}$ phases under high pressure. [22-24,26,31,32]

In hydrogen-rich metal hydrides, the hydrogen-induced volume expansion ($V_{H}$), defined as the volume increase per hydrogen atom added to the metal lattice, is empirically found to be $\sim 1.7-1.8\ \AA^{3}/H(D)$ atom. [12,34-37]

premise.title: Equation of State for Pure Yttrium

premises.premise: The equation of state for pure yttrium describes the pressure dependence of the yttrium atomic volume, allowing for extrapolation to high pressures. [33]

reasoning.step: The authors synthesized body-centred tetragonal ($bct$) $YH_{4}/YD_{4}$ and body-centred cubic ($bcc$) $YH_{6}/YD_{6}$ by compressing $YH_{3}/YD_{3}$ in $H_{2}/D_{2}$ to 160–175 GPa and heating to $\sim$ 1500 K with a pulsed laser.
The authors observed that both $bct\text{-}YH_{4}$ and $bcc\text{-}YH_{6}$ also form by exposing the reactants to higher pressures of $\sim$ 200–244 GPa at room temperature for several weeks.
The authors synthesized the $hcp\text{-}YH_{9}/YD_{9}$ phase by laser heating the previously formed $YH_{4}$ or $YH_{6}$ phases with excess $H_{2}/D_{2}$ at pressures above 185 GPa.

The authors determined the lattice volumes of the synthesized phases ($bct\text{-}YH_{4}$, $bcc\text{-}YH_{6}$, $hcp\text{-}YH_{9}$) via X-ray diffraction and found them to agree well with theoretical predictions [@premise-1], justifying the assignment of the phases.

step.title: Calculation of Hydrogen Volume Expansion for YH4

reasoning.step: Using the extrapolated equation of state for pure yttrium [@premise-3] to isolate the volume of the yttrium sublattice, the authors calculated the hydrogen-induced volume expansion ($V_{H}$) in $bct\text{-}YH_{4}(YD_{4})$ to be $\sim 1.7-1.8\ \AA^{3}/H(D)$ atom at 180–200 GPa.

The authors noted that the calculated $V_{H}$ for $YH_{4}$ ($\sim 1.7-1.8\ \AA^{3}/H(D)$) is comparable to values measured in other hydrogen-rich metal hydrides [@premise-2], unlike $YH_{3}$ which shows an anomalously large expansion.

step.title: Stoichiometry Estimation for bcc and hcp Phases

reasoning.step: Applying the derived hydrogen-induced volume expansion value ($V_{H} \approx 1.7-1.8\ \AA^{3}/H$ atom) to the measured lattice volumes of the new phases, the authors estimated the compositions as $YH_{5.7(3)}$ for the bcc phase and $YH_{8.5(5)}$ for the hcp phase.

inference_unit.conclusion: The authors experimentally determined synthesis pathways for higher yttrium hydrides: - $bct\text{-}YH_{4}$ and $bcc\text{-}YH_{6}$ form at 160–175 GPa via laser heating ($\sim 1500$ K) or at 200–244 GPa at room temperature. - $hcp\text{-}YH_{9}$ forms by laser heating $YH_{6}$ with excess hydrogen above 185 GPa. Based on the hydrogen-induced volume expansion ($V_H \approx 1.7-1.8$ Å$^3$/H atom), the experimental compositions are estimated as $YH_{5.7(3)}$ for the bcc phase and $YH_{8.5(5)}$ for the hcp phase.
